title: ophrys oestrifera subsp. latakiana
intro:
Ophrys oestrifera subsp. latakiana, also known as the bee orchid, is a rare species of orchid found in the Mediterranean region, characterized by its unique appearance and fragrance, which mimics the scent of female bees to attract pollinators.

This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ophrys oestrifera subsp. latakiana include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's extracts can be used to create teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
